data.content:
Implementation plan

Root cause
- Header badge currently uses `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_VERSION` in `src/components/version-badge.tsx`. That value is bundled at build time.
- Update banner uses `/api/version`, backed by `src/lib/release-version.ts`, which reads `RELEASE_VERSION` first and then `package.json`, and also fetches the latest GitHub release.
- On host `adrian`, the runtime can therefore report installed v0.13.3 in the banner while the already-built client bundle still displays v0.13.2 in the badge.

Implementation steps
1. Create implementation worktree from `origin/main`, e.g. `.worktrees/foolery-4f8e-version-source` on branch `codex/4f8e-version-source`, then run `bun install --frozen-lockfile`.
2. Make `/api/version` the canonical UI source for installed/current/latest version state. Keep `src/lib/release-version.ts` as the server-side source of truth.
3. Replace `VersionBadge` compile-time version lookup with a prop-driven/current-status model. Remove `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_VERSION` from `src/components/version-badge.tsx`; pass the installed version through `HeaderToolbar`/`HeaderBranding` from the same version data that drives the banner.
4. Refactor `useVersionBanner` in `src/components/app-header-hooks.ts` into a shared version-status hook that stores `installedVersion`, `latestVersion`, and `updateAvailable`; derive banner visibility from that shared object plus dismissal state.
5. Ensure the badge click/check flow either reuses the same fetch helper or reports results back into the shared version state, so a forced check cannot make the popover and badge disagree.
6. Tighten display formatting so missing versions never render as bare `v`; use an explicit neutral fallback or hide the numeric text until the canonical status is known.
7. Keep manual fallback/update command behavior unchanged.

Tests
- Add focused component/unit coverage proving the badge renders the installed version supplied by the canonical version status rather than an env-bundled value.
- Extend existing version check tests so the parsed API data includes installed/current version and update availability consistently.
- Extend `VersionBannerBar`/header-part tests as needed to prove banner and badge receive the same installed version value.
- Run targeted tests first (`bun run test -- src/components/__tests__/version-badge.test.ts ...`), then full gates: `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, and `bun run build`.

Validation
- If available, verify on host `adrian` after the patch by comparing `/api/version` output with the badge/banner in the running UI.
- No PR workflow. After gates pass, integrate to and push `origin/main` per repo policy.

Complexity and risks
- Complexity: small to medium; mostly client-state prop wiring plus tests.
- Main risks: hydration/flicker while version status loads, duplicate forced checks racing with the initial check, and preserving banner dismissal semantics.
- No child knots are necessary for this scope; it is one cohesive UI contract fix.
